topic.what: First efficient adaptively secure post-quantum DiSE construction using MLWR
topic.how: Constructs MLWR-based adaptively secure DPRF with linear online overheads; proves security in QROM using regularity lemma and programmable RO
topic.why_matters: Bridges gap between quantum-safe DiSE (static security only) and adaptive corruption tolerance; outperforms prior DiSE in online throughput

characteristics.what_it_measures: Encryption/decryption time complexity relative to number of parties
characteristics.vs_baseline: Better than LWE-based O(λt) which requires O(λ) matrix multiplications per partial evaluation
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 shows MLWR-based DPRF requires constant polynomial multiplications per partial evaluation vs λ matrix multiplications for LWE-based
characteristics.context: t = threshold number of parties, T = total parties

characteristics.what_it_measures: Per-party computation cost for DPRF evaluation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Same as LWR-based [35] but with adaptive security (unlike [35] which is static only)
characteristics.evidence: Section 3.2: MLWR-based DPRF requires constant polynomial multiplications per partial evaluation
characteristics.context: Compared to LWE-based [24] which requires O(λ) matrix multiplications

characteristics.vs_baseline: Same exponential as prior lattice-based DPRFs [35,24]
characteristics.evidence: Section 2.3: Each party stores C(T-1,t-1) key shares; Section 4 shows comparison table
characteristics.context: Exponential in threshold parameters but linear online overheads

characteristics.what_itasures: Bit security against classical and quantum adversaries
characteristics.evidence: Section 3.4: N=256, d=4, q=2^64, q1=2^38, p=2^10 provide ≥128-bit security per lattice estimator
characteristics.context: Parameters comparable to Kyber; no known attacks on MLWE/MLWR better than LWE

characteristics.vs_baseline: Outperforms PQDiSE [35] (no adaptive security) and adaptive DiSE [29] (quantum-broken DDH)
characteristics.evidence: Section 5: Experiments show MLWR-based DPRF is faster than AES-based and DDH-based for online evaluation; EQuADiSE scales smoothly to larger (t,T)
characteristics.context: Implementation available at GitHub; scales better than combinatorial AES-based approaches

novelty: First MLWR-based DPRF with QROM adaptive security proof
First DiSE with linear online overheads AND adaptive security AND quantum-safety
New proof technique using regularity lemma for module setting adaptive security
open_problems: Reducing exponential key storage to polynomial
Extending to stronger corruption models beyond semi-honest
Practical threshold FHE integration with adaptive security
